title: quercus havardii
intro: Quercus havardii, also known as Havard's oak, is a deciduous tree species native to North America, specifically found in the mountainous regions of Texas and New Mexico. The bark and leaves of this plant contain tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus havardii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Quercus havardii can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat various conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
